sanskrit:
बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम्।
बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम्॥
transliteration:
bījaṃ māṃ sarva-bhūtānāṃ viddhi pārtha sanātanam |
buddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham ||
literal_meaning: Know Me, O Pārtha, as the eternal seed of all beings; I am the intelligence of the intelligent, the prowess of the powerful.
contextual_meaning: Krishna invites Arjuna to recognize the Ground of Being within every capacity he values—creativity, clarity, strength—so that self-confidence is re-anchored in trans-personal Source rather than ego.
psychological_state: Cognitive re-framing from egoic comparison to participatory consciousness; shift from performance anxiety to intrinsic worth.
trigger_scenario: A top-performing student fears losing rank; remembering this verse steadies her by locating intelligence in the field, not only in her head.
anchor_text: See every talent as a spark of the one limitless Source.
